title: salix setchelliana
intro: Salix setchelliana, also known as Setchell's willow or Setchell's alder, is a species of willow tree native to the Pacific coast of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix setchelliana include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as treating fever and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
